home.hero_title_1: From the field to your table,
home.hero_description: We directly connect consumers with farmers, ranchers and conscious food makers. Proximity, trust and human connection.
home.search_placeholder: What are you looking for? E.g: pistachios, olive oil, truffle...
home.steiner_quote_1: "This is a nutrition problem. Nutrition as understood today…"
home.steiner_quote_2: "…does not provide the strength necessary to manifest the spirit in physical life.
home.steiner_text: Nutrition is not merely material, but a metabolic-rhythmic bridge to the spirit, weakened in conventional farming and restored in biodynamics.
home.values_subtitle: A platform built on values that prioritize people and conscious agriculture above all.
home.value_nonprofit_desc: We charge no commissions and do not intermediate financially. The relationship is always direct between people.
home.value_direct_desc: We facilitate the meeting between consumers and farmers. You decide who to deal with and how.
home.value_biodynamic_desc: We support farming practices that respect the earth, natural cycles and life as a whole.
home.value_transparency_desc: Know the origin of your food. Each farmer freely shares their story and methods.
home.roles_subtitle: A single user can act as consumer, farmer or preparation maker depending on their needs. You decide your role at any time.
home.role_consumer_desc: Find conscious food near you. Connect directly with local farmers and support conscious agriculture.
home.role_farmer_desc: Share your work with consumers who value biodynamic agriculture. No commissions, direct contact.
home.role_elaborador_desc: Offer biodynamic preparations (500-508, Maria Thun) to farmers looking to practice biodynamics.
home.preps_description: Biodynamic preparations are special substances that vitalize soil and plants. We connect farmers with makers so you can access these preparations directly.
home.disclaimer_p1: Alimentos Conscientes is a simple, free directory so that conscious farmers and mindful consumers can find each other. We don't sell anything, we don't intermediate — we only facilitate the meeting between people.
home.disclaimer_p2: All your data belongs to you. At any time you can download all your information in JSON format from your profile. This way, if this directory integrates with any association or platform in the future, you can take your entire product catalogue with you.
home.disclaimer_p3: By signing up and publishing your data (name, location, products), you accept that this information will be publicly visible in the directory so consumers and other farmers can find you. That's the essence of the directory: being visible to connect.
home.disclaimer_p4: This platform is also open to organic farmers who want to learn about biodynamic practices. Here you can discover what biodynamic agriculture is, connect with preparation makers, and take the step towards a more conscious form of farming connected to the earth.
home.cta_description: Whether you are a consumer, farmer, rancher or preparation maker, here you have a space to connect directly and transparently.

preparations_page.title: Biodynamic preparations
preparations_page.subtitle: Preparations 500-508 are the heart of biodynamic agriculture, formulated by Rudolf Steiner in 1924. They vitalize soil and plants.
preparations_page.prep_500_subtitle: Cow horn filled with manure, buried in autumn
preparations_page.prep_500_desc: The foundational preparation for the soil. A cow horn is filled with fresh manure and buried during winter. When unearthed in spring, the contents have transformed into dark, aromatic humus. Stirred in water and sprayed on soil, it stimulates microbial life, humus formation and root development.
preparations_page.prep_501_subtitle: Ground quartz in cow horn, buried in spring
preparations_page.prep_501_desc: The luminous complement to 500. Finely ground quartz is placed in a cow horn and buried during summer. Stirred and sprayed as a fine mist over plants, it enhances photosynthesis, improves nutritional quality and flavor, and strengthens the plant's relationship with light.
preparations_page.prep_502_desc: Yarrow flowers wrapped in a deer bladder. Brings sensitivity to sulphur and potassium, helping the plant attract nutritive substances in minimal but essential quantities. Added to the compost pile.
preparations_page.prep_503_subtitle: Matricaria chamomilla — Calcium and soil life
preparations_page.prep_503_desc: Chamomile flowers wrapped in cow intestine and buried during winter. Regulates calcium processes and stabilizes nitrogen in compost, promoting healthy and balanced decomposition of organic matter.
preparations_page.prep_504_desc: Stinging nettle buried directly in the earth for one year. Brings nutritional intelligence to the soil: regulates iron, sensitizes the earth to cosmic influences and helps nutrients become available to plants in a balanced way.
preparations_page.prep_505_desc: Crushed oak bark placed inside a domestic animal skull, buried in a wet location. Provides living calcium forces that strengthen soil structure and help prevent fungal diseases in crops.
preparations_page.prep_506_subtitle: Taraxacum officinale — Silica and cosmic sensitivity
preparations_page.prep_506_desc: Dandelion flowers wrapped in cow mesentery and buried during winter. Connects the earth with cosmic silica forces, helping plants pick up subtle influences from their environment. Stimulates the relationship between soil and atmosphere.
preparations_page.prep_507_desc: Liquid extract of valerian flowers. The only preparation applied as a liquid over the compost. Stimulates phosphorus processes and creates a protective layer of warmth over the compost pile, like a living thermal blanket.
preparations_page.prep_508_subtitle: Equisetum arvense — Silica and fungal protection
preparations_page.prep_508_desc: Horsetail decoction. Used as a preventive spray against fungi and cryptogamic diseases. Its high silica content regulates watery forces and protects plants from excess moisture.
preparations_page.prep_maria_thun_subtitle: Barrel compost preparation by Maria Thun
preparations_page.prep_maria_thun_desc: Developed by Maria Thun as a practical alternative. Cow manure layered with preparations 502-507 and left to mature. Allows applying the forces of all compost preparations directly to soil, ideal for those without their own compost.
preparations_page.cta_auth_title: Want to see which makers offer these preparations?
preparations_page.cta_auth_desc: Sign up for free to see prices and contact them directly.
preparations_page.cta_elab_desc: Sign up as a preparation maker and offer them to the community.

about.title: About Us
about.hero_desc: We are a non-profit community connecting people who grow, make and consume conscious food.
about.why_p1: Biodynamic agriculture has existed since 1924, when Rudolf Steiner offered his Agricultural Course to a group of farmers concerned about soil degradation and food quality.
about.why_p2: A hundred years later, finding biodynamic food is still difficult. Farmers who practice this form of cultivation often work in isolation, without a simple way to show what they produce. And consumers looking for these foods don't know where to turn.
about.why_p3: Alimentos Conscientes was born to solve this: a simple, free, no-commission directory where farmers, ranchers and preparation makers can make themselves known, and where anyone can find them.
about.why_p4: We don't sell anything. We don't intermediate. We just connect people.
about.value_1_desc: This project has no commercial owner. It belongs to everyone. No commissions, no advertising, no data sold.
about.value_2_desc: You decide who you talk to and how. We only facilitate the meeting.
about.value_3_desc: Biodynamic agriculture is not just another label. It's a way of understanding the relationship between humans, the earth and the cosmos.
about.value_4_desc: The code is open. The data is yours. You can export it whenever you want.
about.steiner_p1: In June 1924, Rudolf Steiner gave eight lectures in Koberwitz (now Kobierzyce, Poland) that laid the foundations of biodynamic agriculture: a way of farming that sees the farm as a living organism.
about.steiner_p2: Biodynamic preparations (500-508) are the heart of this method: natural substances that vitalize soil and plants, made following nature's rhythms.
about.steiner_p3: Today, biodynamic agriculture is practiced in more than 50 countries. In Spain, it grows little by little thanks to committed farmers who seek a different way of relating to the earth.
about.cta_desc: Whether you're a farmer, consumer or preparation maker, this is your place.
